lesson.title: Sets, Relations, and Functions
lesson.explanation: Sets are collections of distinct elements. Operations: union (A∪B), intersection (A∩B), difference (A\B), complement (Aᶜ). A relation R on set A is a subset of A×A. Relations can be reflexive (aRa), symmetric (aRb → bRa), transitive (aRb ∧ bRc → aRc). An equivalence relation (all three) partitions a set into equivalence classes. Functions map each input to exactly one output.
